I think you're really really fun This helpful right so at the essence is this is this idea that in this the sales training world Right they always talk about these a you know two models of selling in terms of there being an old model of selling and then there's a new model of selling and They're very different things now in reality The concept itself is total fucking nonsense It's grounded in bullshit and the mostly self-serving of the self-serving variety with people who want to like sort of you know Put a new twist on the sales process and say oh this is you know room They charge you for right so I want us first to mystify it and then explains you the truth about the whole matter Right so basically the drawer down for you know how awesome. I am when it comes to my no artistic, you know Wow is it right so? Number one, okay The old and new model is based on the fact that the old model is the sort of hard charging You know grab your throat close close close ABC always be closing type of sales person, right? So if we use a pie chart here to explain it so you have let's say in the model of The old way you would have what's called they call rapport building report call wrap rapport building and they say you should spend 10% of your time Building rapport and getting into rapport Okay, then they say you should spend Let's see how ugly this gets here 20% of your time 20% of your time Going through questions meaning you're qualifying your qualifying questions So you want to find out your clients needs Values right whatever it might be Qualifying right so you ask questions they answer to understand a if someone could afford your product. They need your product So forth right Then you spend 30% of your time This is actually pretty good by the way 30% of your time Presenting so in other words essentially you spend 20% of the time finding out really what your prospect needs 30% of your time Presenting to that need solving that problem so to speak right and then you spend the vast majority of your time closing the deal Close close close right that's the old model of selling okay, and it Sort of like what you would see in the movie Glenn Gary Glenn Ross Boiler even the wolf of Wall Street. That's the fit. It's a fictitious label. What was going on there? Because it's nothing like that okay, but that's what you would think of right on the other side of the equation you have let's call the new Model and the new models really known as like the Consultative model of selling and and let me explain to you what really consultative selling is about Consultative selling is for people who are scared to say they're salespeople. I'm not really a salesperson I just talk to people and fill their needs. It's like a fucking joke. Okay, so the whole Concept of consultative selling is to trick people into thinking that you're not really selling anybody anything You're a consultant. You're just a friend. You're an you're the analyst type You're not a salesperson. You're not an influencer and it's this ridiculous notion But let me just tell you how they break it out. He's only sort of they sort of do a semantic kind of dance here Okay, so with this new model. Let's take the same pie chart here. Okay now You see my town come out here with the fucking wap side of pie chart. Okay, so here if you add 10% of your time We were poor they say here you spent 40% of your time It's my mark here 40% of your time what they call building relationships Which is basically the poor by another name, but they call it building relationships, right? Then they say you spent 30% of your time 30% this is really bad by the way 30% of your time There's a time. Okay, um Yeah, essentially gathering intelligence, right? Gathering intelligence you spend 20 or say what they would be identifying needs. Let's call identifying needs That's the real label identifying needs then you spend 20% of your time offering solutions And This should be 10% you say I told you I suck at this 10% of your time I promise to graphic my guys creators like a billion times better this 10% of your time. Okay confirming and Closing in other words a Consultative salesman doesn't have to close a constant sales doesn't have to answer objections They just simply spend so much time getting to know people and building trust and They Understand their prospects so well. There's simply no need to close It's the idea that you can front-load a sale. So remember in straight line terminology, right? We talk about the front half and the back half right even though they're not really has we use it as a Just a figure of speech in the front half of the sale. Okay represents your taking control of the sale Right using that control to gather intelligence and build massive rapport. You have your transition You then make us you present your solution your presentation your solution, right? And then you ask for the order for the first time That's the front half of the sale then when you get to that point there's three possibilities Well according to consultative selling It's really never gonna be many objections because they just love you so much you spend so much time building that relationship You only 10% I just gotta tell you something in the real world of selling If you try this you'll be fucking broke and destitute and out on the street It's a complete lie and a myth. All right, and I'm not saying that someone who teaches a consultative type of model Can't be effective, but they're just lying to you and they say that you're not closing It's nonsense. Okay, and it probably teaches you the straight line disguise anyway Because the only way you can close is by using a straight line pad and all the They all come down to that in the end. That's what's really happening But the point is if you only spent 10% of your time closing You'd never close. It's not possible. Let me just tell you flat out as Ridiculous as 10% is it's just as ridiculous to say that you spend 40% of your time closing You want to know what percentage of the time I spent closing a hundred percent I Am closing every moment I'm speaking from the moment my prospect lays eyes on me in person Moment they hear my voice over the phone. I'm fucking closing Am I closing them on buying my product at that point? No, not exactly I'm closing them that I'm a person worth listening to That I can help them achieve their goals now backing that up a step. What does that mean? Well, I'm closing them on the fact that I'm an expert in my field that I'm sharp I'm on the ball and the problem is all I'm enthusiastic what I have must be good I'm closing them on that from the moment I open up my mouth because I know that if I don't successfully close them on Feeling that way about me guess what they will take control of the sale and then things spiral out of control you cannot Take someone down the straight line when they're in control it becomes the crooked line It becomes the every sale is different scenario the basic concepts of the straight line system Every sale is the same. What does that really mean? Well, it doesn't mean that everyone says the same words It doesn't mean that everyone has the same belief systems the same pain points the same values Hit you with the same objections doesn't mean that They'll those will all be different, but what always remains the same is That at the end of the day There are certain core elements that must line up in your prospects mind in any prospects mind Before you have any shot of closing them now. There are three for sure Three for sure But there's typically more usually there's five and it depends on what the person's action threshold is There's it's also very low action threshold Well, the reality that number is pretty much already taken get that that element is been already resolved You don't have to do it's not it's already been addressed because the person has beliefs that say, you know I just I love to buy shit. I make quick decisions I trust people so that numbers out of the equation and that pretty much takes pain based out of the equation as well for the Most part because you can handle enough pain at the front that you don't really need to reintroduce pay the end So no, it's the five. Let's go back. I'm assuming here since you're on this program, right? Now you understand what the five four elements. Oh, let me list them real quickly. So what these five core elements are is number one Before someone's gonna buy from you before they're gonna say yes, they have to love your product People don't buy things that they think are just okay People don't buy things that they think suck They don't buy things that they will make their lives worse. Those human beings by by our very nature We always app operate. This is a very important point guys, so write this down You got a pen in front of your phone write this down Human beings at all but at the end on nature. We always operate from a position of positive intent meaning we make decisions that We believe and the opera word is believe that we intend to improve our lives When we say yes to buying something is because we've said, yeah, I think this will make my life better not worse We don't buy things that will make our lives worth whether we think will make our lives worse You get it you present some with an insurance policy or a new car or a Software program and when they're running that through the mental computer Hmm. No, I think that thing is gonna actually make things more complicated or it's gonna put me at Morris It'll make me feel worse about my future There's no shot. No fucking shot. They'll buy they will not buy We don't buy things that we think will make our lives worse We buy things that we think will make our lives better and again the word is think Because very often We're wrong people are wrong. There's a lot of serial bad decision makers out there Who make a lot of wrong decisions, but at the time they thought they were right based on positive intent for example if someone decides against buying your product In the moment they were saying, you know, I don't believe this. It's not I don't think it's gonna help me They could probably be wrong. It actually is an amazing product that will benefit their lives tremendously But because of positive intent, they don't buy I mean look at another way. No one Thinks about it says well, let me think about this. So if I buy this thing I really fucked my life up and make me keep me up at night and make me feel like an idiot. Okay, I'll take it You get it like that. It's just logic guys, okay So let's let's not you try to make pretty fancy coatings or labels people don't buy shit. I think sucks They buy things that they think are awesome. It will make their lives better. They'll be fun. So let's say a number of 10 is absolute certainty We're there absolutely certain and damn sure of it best thing in the world A one means it is the biggest piece of shit I would never be called dead wearing that outfit driving that car using that computer It's fucking a rip-off. They give shit customer service and fucking smells. It's ugly. It won't work So what's happening here is that when you're moving someone down the lake down the shoreline you're corralling these core elements The first is what they must love the product, right because of positive intent or else they won't buy number two, they must trust and connect with you and again Positive intent is this because they future pace. Well, if I think by allowing this person into my life My life's gonna get really crappy Oh, yeah, okay. Yeah, I'll sign up. No one would do it It always comes down to positive intent at the end of the day The the bitch of it all was that just because someone thinks something is bad doesn't mean it is or if they think it's good Doesn't mean it is as well yet people can be really bad decision makers So our job by the way is to essentially make sure because positive intent is almost moves in direct lockstep with the certainty scale How positive someone feels about making a decision is based on how certain they are the more certain they are the more You're in the right side of positive intent And this is what I kind of picked up on but I this is why I never really spoke about this so much before It's a very important point to make because it clarifies really what you're doing as a salesperson And why you can't move forward in a sale on the straight line if someone's not really sure about your product or you or your company You you haven't established enough certainty to make sure positive intent doesn't get in the way because everything else could line up But if there's no positive intent people say let me think about they don't say sorry. I'm not sure it makes about like that They won't say that they'll just say let me think about when they call you back and they use smoke screens as objections So these are your first three elements trust you trust the company And there are two more that come behind it four is your action threshold Five is your pain threshold So these last two Don't always come into play Well on some level pain is typically So this pain even a little bit of pain is enough to lubricate the wheels those so whether someone's a buyer in heat Or a buyer in power Will be related to how much pain they're feeling there's a buyer in heat So it's hot the product they're feeling some pain Some lack in their life they feel you get that and that's what makes them a buyer in heat a buyer in power says Yeah, I need the product Want the product My room is like, can you open that door please? It's getting really hot in here. Just open if you open the door out there too Let's get a draft in it. It's just way too hot in here I'm gonna get the house white. I'm on the beach here. So we don't air conditioning anymore, but I'm gonna get ac in spokes It's not possible going to the summer, right? Thank you Thanks guys. All right. So anyway, you get that So as you move someone down the line, right That this idea of is that you're getting past the point of someone's action threshold meaning the collective Level of certainty That someone needs to be at Before they're comfortable enough to say yes, like again for me my beliefs about buying Making decisions How I process fear my Feeling of self-reliance that I can make good decisions quickly I'm not worried about what others think of me so much, right? That leads to a very low action threshold So if like let's say i'm in a 6.5 Will I buy I might buy actually But if i'm in a 4.5, I will never buy Oh, yeah, I'll yeah, let me fuck my life up some more and you understand Positive intent is the fucking magic bullet that drives everything now What happens though is remember for those people who have beliefs that are very much different than mine They're like my best decision is a slow decision. I don't trust salespeople I like to think things through those are all beliefs that fall into high action threshold category Someone who possesses those beliefs Is gonna have to be Absolutely certain about all three tens and damn sure of it. Otherwise, you know what they'll say They'll say hey, let me think about it have to be so Again this idea of well how what I say every sales the same Well number one those three things Always come with these firstly always come into play any one of them is missing You have negative positive intent and you you're fucked. They just it's it's a monkey wrench and it It stops the press from saying yes, that's one side of the equation then for those people who have inherently high action thresholds right where they like to think things through they don't like making quick decisions They don't trust salespeople those people You're gonna have to attack that fourth number that action threshold and lower it Very easy to do And then if that doesn't work you could also add on more pain Reestablish their pain they can step into it talk about it expand on it and elevate it and by By just the way it works the by definition The way a pain threshold works is an inverse relationship with action threshold as you add on pain The action threshold drops I always tell a story about my dad This call loved this car. No one could touch his car, but fucking jimmy at the snow station, right? Well one day we were 200 miles from home car breaks down So gilliet night is worried his family's in danger. What does it do? It goes to the first gas station. Why pain fear worry Pain fear worry those sorts of emotions they will essentially collapse Someone's action threshold in the moment. So my daddy. Yeah, no one touches my car, but jimmy Unless I'm 200 miles from home and I'm worried sick of my cars broken down and I think these people are gonna come and rape my family Well, then he'll go to the first mechanic he can find That's the reality Now it's a very extreme situation, right? But there's so many things we could do through language patterns There'll be awful things through scarcity that create a very similar response reaction without having to go that far And once you add on that pain So once you add by adding on pain It lowers the action threshold. Here's another way of looking at imagine A scale, right? I'm saying a longer stick here. Is there a sticker? I mean, it was my tennis racket I'll just use this right. Let's say this is a look make this a longer pig, right? And the scale's got a fulcrum in the middle My scale's got a fulcrum, right? This is the fulcrum. So the scale's balanced, right? All right, this is your fulcrum in the middle now What happens is if the fulcrum is in the middle, right? And you add a weight to this a one pound weight here and a one pound weight there. What happens? They're even but if the fulcrum is over here, let's say the fulcrum is on the right side Okay, you have one pound and one pound what happens So these are your negatives. These are your positives here your negatives here If you have a high action threshold It's like having the fulcrum all the way on this side of the scale Each positive doesn't give that much weight to things because the fulcrum is over here On the other side one little negative Tips to scale they don't buy from you When you add on pain what it does is it brings the fulcrum back into the center And it was a buy that's what happens the same thing goes even when you're just lowering the action threshold Using that language pattern. What's the worst that can possibly have? Essentially by taking this is your fulcrum here, right? And your focus over here and then you say what's the worst? And not that much your upsides, you know is massive your downsides. You make them run different moves It brings the folk from back in the middle and people buy So when I say every sale is the same What I'm Meaning here is that those five elements must line up At least three and if the first three are not enough then you've got to raise up Their pain threshold and lower their action threshold all five elements, right now The only possible way That you can do this effectively Is if you're in control of the sale If you're not in control of the conversation, you're screwed You then every sale truly is different because there's you cannot go about lining those five things up in a strategic way You see I know when I walk into an encounter I know exactly the order in which i'm going to Essentially line up those five elements I know exactly because i'm doing it the same way Every time my prospects might say different things to me today. I have different values different pain points different objections But those five elements as I overcome all other stuff Those five elements Are always being lined up in the same order and using the same strategies because He perceives me as an expert or she perceives me as an expert as a figure of authority as a force To be reckoned with so what do they do? They say okay, you're an expert show me how okay? You're not the real deal and they'll make reporting. You understand this this this Nonsense that there's a partition with what you're doing. We saw it. It's just not true You're always doing all things at once with and and by the way without that You know what happened you break all the rules of influence and you don't close That's what happens when you try to segment stuff out into boxes You inadvertently break rule after rule of influence and you're closed at the low You know you close people with really low actions as we buy from anyone You don't close the ones who are tougher and that's what real salesmen do and that's why they get paid so much Top producers close the ones that others feel are difficult. They know how to do it And they are always Aware that every word and their mouth is being judged analyzed hang on. There are no free words And then getting back to the end of it the actual you know, I said close them in You know, listen, it's just nonsensical Okay As a salesperson you're doing all things at once and that brings us back to this whole notion of abc Always be closing the what people mistake about this. They think that abc is about that you're always Closed like you're always trying to get to buy from you. No, it's not what it means abc means that you're always Closing them on whatever that step of the process is at that moment because as soon as you stop doing that you're done Okay, remember these two models Are both nonsense because you're always doing all things at once and rather than it being about what am I building before? It's about following a pathway to line up the core elements of influence And we know if we do that people will buy from us That's the important point here. Okay next I want to switch that now. I'm just doing a completely different topic. Okay. I want to talk about something on the inner game Which has been coming up a lot lately. Okay. I did I did a podcast yesterday, right? Um, a Tuesday yesterday two days ago and it was I won't mention with Hooper's with one of the biggest podcasters in the world One of the biggest podcasts, right? And you guys know I'm launching my own podcast by like next two weeks or three weeks. It'll be out It's gonna be awesome. It's called the wolves then you definitely want to subscribe I'll show you a will to me hysterical It's gonna be a snowflake fucking free zone and there's no safe spaces here The fucking wolf will be on the attack bearing fangs. Anyway point being Um, I did this podcast and one of the things that really came up was belief systems about beliefs I think it was maybe a year ago I'm sure very few of you have seen me do this. I went through this this um chart That I referred to as the cycle of beliefs How beliefs get formed? And how they end up tripping you up. So let me just back up and explain, you know, what a belief really is A belief is essentially it's something that you think Is true It's something it's something you think to be true I think I'm great at I believe I'm great at tennis. I believe in the world. I think it's true It's not a fact beliefs are not facts of life Beliefs are the things about the world about ourselves that we think to be true You have something called identity beliefs I'm too old I'm too young I'm too fat. I'm too thin. I'm handsome. I'm ugly. I'm gregarious and outgoing. I'm alone I'm a I'm a introvert beliefs. There's all fucking beliefs. There's nothing empirically right or wrong. They're belief systems now On the flip side the question is well, what causes beliefs to be formed Well, at the end of the day What really forms beliefs are these root? experiences that we have That serve as is almost like it starts with a root experience And then once something happens to us that experience Changes the way we perceive things and we start actually cutting out things that don't agree with this belief And we narrow and narrow our field of focus and we only see things and notice things that line up with our beliefs And we delete things that don't That's how it works. Now I'm going to get back to that in a second Let me just give you the other type of beliefs to the other type one was identity. The second is capability beliefs What you're capable of I am capable of being getting rich I'm capable of making my own way in life. I'm capable of closing anyone who's closeable I'm capable of massive compassion I'm capable of being a great public speaker. If you don't think you'd be a great public speaker. I got new So you guess what you know, you ain't gonna want to do public speaking if you don't think you can close You're not going to try to close but you don't you don't try things that we think we're not good at We don't try we don't go we don't go for the brass ring If we don't believe we're gonna get that brass ring, you know, why it makes sense because again, it's almost positive intent the brain says What's the point? Why do all the work? Why should I go and spend? 100 to or a thousand man hours and when I really know my heart of hearts. I don't really have what it takes I'm not really that sort of guy or girl. I don't really possess all the requisite skills. That's just I don't really see myself succeeding It's the capability belief right now. Let me show you this chart and believes here and how it works So I'll try my hand at being in this world class honestly again. It's by the way, there's a reason I'm super terrible honest So watch here's the way it works You have four boxes And essentially this first box is what we have is called pure potential Meaning it's like we're born on day one and we're perfect now. I understand some people are Got to belt a very bad debt and their physical you know Limitations really and there are those people this does not refer to that right so if you god god Hope is that you're not that person but if you have a mentally challenged in some way You know or you're physically challenged and you want to become a basketball player you get it So this reality here too, but when I'm talking about the general way we move through the world, right on day one We have generally pure potential Right, that's day one. They were born then comes day two of our lives, right after our parents start to fucking take it away Because here's what happens You start with pure potential and then as you start moving through life you take actions okay Okay, and based on those actions right here you take actions, right? And based on those actions, you are going to get what you're going to get results Both positive and negative and then based on those results You're going to form a belief about who you are and what you're capable of for example Let's say you go out and you're in third grade And the teacher says to you okay. I want you to come Jimmy time for you to give your speech in front of the class now And Jimmy stands up. He's nervous because no one really taught Jimmy had a do public speaking Jimmy didn't prepare himself. He hasn't he didn't really write out his speech He doesn't have his coherent thoughts and assertions to make Jimmy's about to go up and wing it and he's not one of those people who was I was like that who could wing it and do great Jimmy is not a natural born closer or speaker. So what happens? Jimmy's average so Jimmy walks up in front of the room his heart's beating a mile a minute So what he's supposed to do And as he starts talking the kids like And everyone starts making faces and Jimmy starts to feel pain and uncomfortability. Jimmy got a negative result Got a very poor result As Jimmy watches the people snicker and laugh at him. He feels pain inside emotional hurt, right? And he forms a belief saying what? Well, number one people are mean but two I'm just not good at public speaking I'm not I'm not I'm not a good speaker. I'm not good. I hate and he goes and then he goes I hate public speaking. I never want to do this again and Jimmy starts to develop a fear of public speaking at actual limitation because what happens is because of this belief and the fear Associating what happens as he goes back to do it again. His potential was diminished because next time Fresh with the memory of the disaster of last time what happens? Well, people typically do not it's very rare that some will actually practice something they suck at and Unless they're really being pushed by someone in which case it's a very good thing But what happens typically is the next time Jimmy says I I'm not good at that. I don't want to do that again He won't prepare. He won't study. He won't try to overcome it. All right. And next time he comes up. He's paralyzed even more So what happens to his was takes the action again his results even worse What happens his belief is reinforced. I am really bad. I suck. I'm the worst Let me give you a bad example here with me I'm the I'm a terrible artist, right? I really am right. Let me ask you an honest question You know heart to heart do you think that I'm missing an art gene Is there something wrong with me? I'm a pretty talented person normally, right? Am I missing the gene that I just simply can't draw a line. I can't draw a bowl of fruit I can't paint that is there something wrong with me. Am I deficient and so was my dna fucked up No It's not at all. Let me tell you what happened to me. I'll tell you exactly what happened When I was in kindergarten I had this teacher named mrs. Roskin. All right and mrs. Roskin She was a nice little plumpy mrs. Roskin, right? And she had a bowl of fruit and it was time for people to do painting and crayons, right? One of the interesting interesting things that came up is that Is that what really happens with tonality is people And I didn't realize this until I was you know started training salesman is that when I Was out there in the early days Sadly myself. I had just unbelievable tonality. It's natural. It's naturally great with tonality. Perfect tonality Perfect right on right? I began to notice though That a lot of people at awful tonality They had terrible tonality They didn't like monotone. They're flat. They just couldn't get it. So when I start to train salesmen I'd say okay guys I say like okay name of the company and I'd say now you say that And they'd say name of the company. No, no, no, no, you don't like no I want you to sound absolutely certain the guy said name of the company Like wait, dude, that's not certain That's not sir. I want you to sound I want to say name of the company arrow times making up words here Arrow time international cutting edge. I take from I want you to say name of the company Something wrong with their ear You understand that there's a flaw They they think they sound a certain way and they don't and even when I press them and I press them and I press them to keep You don't sound like they won't do it What I ultimately figured out was a shortcut To help people who are really struggling with tonality and that shortcut is using your body Physiology to help you elicit certain tonalities for instance There was a girl who I was training on tonality on stage And I said I want you to lower your voice into a whisper and I want you to create scarcity here And I kept saying now Jim the reason for the call today Something just came across my desk and she'd be Jim the reason for the call today. I'm like, no, no lower your voice She gave Jim the reason she couldn't fucking do it Now I know that might seem outlandish But it might be you just because you wouldn't realize it if it were you People struggle with this shit and it rips them apart when selling those tonalities everything, right? You know what I did to to to fix it I said I want you to do this. I mean I think it was Cindy. It's Cindy. I want you to narrow your eyes It's just a slits Okay, crinkle up your nose and I want you to say the same words She goes well, Jim the reason it's sure enough. She lowers her voice Into a whisper. She goes well reason You can use your body to manifest certain tonalities. It's unbelievable. Watch what I say Jim Certain certainty I say Jim all I'm asking for You'll see me do this all time utter Sound fair enough Now I could say sound fair enough or sound fair enough sound I could muster tonality in my sleep with any physiology, but let me tell you some most people can't they have to be congruent Their body has to match their tonality So you can actually use your body to create the tonalities that you're struggling with and I go through this in the in the cert program And I want to do it right now with you. Here's how you do it. So you you break up with with a partner and You put an a and a b. Okay, you can have the a be the person with less hair and b's won't wire, right? Okay, you know who you are you bald bastards. Okay So anyway, so you break up into a group and then you pick a topic that's out tuna fish And you say I want you to talk about tuna fish or This wall or this light. I want you to talk about that And I want you to just do it in a way of just fucking over the top certainty I want you to be absolutely certain about this this wall Now when you the reason I tell you to pick a topic that's ridiculous Because what it does it's short circuits the floor in your communication If I try to tell you to sound certain something really should sound certain Now you won't be able to you'll link back your old shit to it But I tell you to do about something stupid it somehow short circuits give a what's happening a poor connection Your brain is not linked up the right way where you can access tonalities and words together So by creating stupid words, okay, it confuses your brain It's not scrambles your brain and frees you up to create a tonality you want you can actually consciously let it out So I say look this tuna fish is the best tuna fish tool now I want you to pick something stupid like that and then I want so it's been 30 to 45 seconds Getting absolutely certain about tuna fish while the other person watches them And then after that 30 45 seconds the person who's watching is okay when you are sounding certain You move your arms like this your hands will like this your eye and tell them how their physiology was positioned And then what happens is you can then then take that physiology and apply it and also the right tonality comes out You do that for each of the 10 core influencing tonalities and one by one you start locking these down That's the gift that keeps on giving you get it Very simple process So then you go the a's first and the b's and you go one by one tonality through tonality Until you got every single one of those tonality locked out like for instance The one that to me that is that I said before this girl was narrowing your eyes When as soon as you know what happens you automatically Oh, Jim you almost can't it's almost like you can't keep your eyes narrowed and talk in a loud voice It's too incongruent. You're like Rich in reason You can judge it's I can't you know, I can't do that. Okay So well, yeah, you know, you shouldn't again dan then there's that that lead generation is just about finding people Okay, who who potentially could use your product need your product and have interest in your product You're not looking to close them other than you're closing them on that They'll accept getting information from you and they'll accept that you can ask them questions They'll answer your questions, right? So I wouldn't try to do that I'd hold most of my bullets in harness right now give them enough just so they you know It's more of an impulse to answer questions if you sound great and then you call them back That's when you really go through your antilogical case or if you send them a brochure and you know You know over the internet and email brochure or something that direct them to a website Well there you start building the emotional the logical case by what you sent them But not on the call in the first you know on the phone the first question. Terry forward says if I can ask a second question Does JB have any advice on how to shorten longer sales cycles? Three to six month deals specifically payment devices and security tech is my industry Listen again, you know, I'm always trying to Shorten the sale cycle collapse it. There's some aspects of any sale cycle that um It's just the realities of going through the various levels of decision makers and corralling everyone's certainty The way to really do this is to just test Collapsing less calls less touch points and do it that way in other words And you gotta just test it and get you know, you always try to you always try to get it down to the shortest Cycle the slowest number of calls until you find you're in a point of diminishing returns where by doing that your closing rate starts to drop You agree? All right next question one more. We've got about three more guys All right, Edgar says cube. Oh well. He's asking me But what are four sides in context of boxing someone in when exactly do you know this is he's just asking me to give to you Okay, good. All right. What are the four sides in context of boxing someone in when exactly do you know when you have blocked every exit ramp? So so boxing someone is is a term I don't really use much anymore because it has a negative connotation It wasn't meant to be that way. But you know, you know over time you just you know learn to say things more elegantly, right? You know boxing someone in what it really means is that You are using consistency At a very high level the consistency is one of Robert Sheldini's core Influencing, um, you know, it's a linchpin for influence Consistency scarcity reciprocity authority Likeability social proof those are skilled Steldini's um influences, right? consistency means that people Want to remain consistent with things they've said out loud and things they've done So when someone says oh, I really love this then you have a much better shot of them saying I now might as well buy it First don't say I hate that. I think this thing sucks. Then once they say that it's like digging the heels in the sand The lowest level of consistency is you know, when you say to someone Get it say you'll get them into the habit of saying yes Is it by somehow doing that then when you ask for the order they'll say yes It's just not true But you certainly wouldn't want to get so into the habit of saying no But it's that's a very low level of it, right? So in terms of consistency. It's a very powerful thing Boxing someone in is when you say to so do you say to someone? Well, Jim, let me ask you a question. Does the idea make sense to you? Do you like the idea and they say yeah, it sounds pretty good? That's not a 10 and then it's a say. Oh, exactly. It really is a great idea. In fact, let me say this I loop back I give a second pattern And now after the second pattern and I say you see what I'm saying Jim. Do you like oh, no? Yeah, it's great. Oh my god. Yeah I say exactly And then as I keep moving down the line I'll start doing the same thing for the second 10 to third 10 and each time they keep saying no I'm looping back creating more certainty and more certainty what's happening is as I keep getting say. Yeah, if I knew you Yes, if I knew you I'd be doing it right now I love the product you I love the product And I could see myself in the future feeling great doing a box because the sides of the box I love the prology feel myself in the future in the future is more that's a box, right? And then they say but I don't trust you then you cut that out Let me tell you more about myself and you and you say then you make them trust you at your forest gun pattern And then trust the company on the bottom. It's large. It's it's that it's what you're doing is is that you're getting people say, yeah I if I knew you Yes I understand the product is awesome. I don't know you well enough. I say I get that the box is then now Now let me tell you about myself and when they're done They're like, oh and then you get to say oh my that sounds all they start to become they almost have to a certain point You said, you know jim. Let's say I'm wrong Okay, and you have to and you and the product doesn't work as well you cash in your guarantee You have zero loss that he'll say yeah on the upside though If it's even half right you saw the future pace it gets to a point where there's no really Reasonable reason to say reasonable reason There's no reason that's reasonable for them to say let me think you're taking away their they almost have to go against their own belief systems their own prior statements their own Anchors what they think is proper decision making and normal human interactions because of doing the right thing by other than Right is you're living every possibility why someone could rationally say no and feel good about it That's boxing someone in but I don't want you to do that in an unethical. It's not unethical. This is just sales That's what you do when you sell when you sell you're just making this such a great case to them and Eliminating these bullshit sort of excuses that people have they just smoke screens They're not really thinking through and you and you bring it to the forefront you get them. So you know what I really just I just don't I don't really have that's exactly what happened with the guy on the go back to the dairy sale It's a perfect example of boxing someone in I made such an airtight logical case such an airtight emotional case Had him so sold on me and like Where was he gonna go? Just remember this don't get so caught up in this. Does the idea make sense to you? It doesn't always fit like it's not it's not about that That's an anchor phrase I used to explain to you the first thing is the product sometimes it'll work Sometimes not just remember what you're doing Notice you're looping around and around and sometimes you deflect the first objection, right? But it's not always me while I hear what you're saying It's just it's common sometimes so it's not the point is is that you're always essentially running this basic very simple Strategy of I deflect the first objection. I find out how certain they are about my product I then make them a lot more certain make I do a certainty check for myself the company asks for the order again Next I'll do another loop back. I'll do the same thing and then lower their act It's really easy guys. Okay.
